text: The surgeon aspirates fluid from a right-sided ovarian cyst using an aspiration needle, collecting 5 ml for cytopathology. The cyst opening is enlarged to introduce suction, allowing complete removal of the cystic fluid. The inner cystic layer is held with one instrument while the outer layer is stripped away with another instrument, demonstrating efficient tissue separation.

The surgeon ensures complete separation of the cystic valve down to the base. The telescope is repositioned to the left iliac fossa port, and the cyst is extracted through the 10 mm umbilical port using a cloth.
